req.title: Bulk data export (audit)
req.statement: A provisioned client shall request a FHIR bulk export; large datasets shall be paginated with NDJSON output. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
pytest tests for FHIR Bulk Export requirements:
  1. A provisioned client shall request a FHIR bulk export.
  2. Large datasets shall be paginated with NDJSON output.
  3. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation, tested end-to-end.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import time
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Generator
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

import httpx
import ndjson
import pytest
import responses as responses_lib
from responses import RequestsMock

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants / helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

BASE_URL = "https://fhir.example.com"
BULK_EXPORT_KICK_OFF = f"{BASE_URL}/$export"
EXPORT_STATUS_URL = f"{BASE_URL}/$export-status"
AUDIT_EVENT_URL = f"{BASE_URL}/AuditEvent"
TOKEN_URL = f"{BASE_URL}/oauth/token"

NDJSON_CONTENT_TYPE = "application/fhir+ndjson"
FHIR_CONTENT_TYPE = "application/fhir+json"

CLIENT_ID = "test-client-id"
CLIENT_SECRET = "test-client-secret"  # noqa: S105 (test secret)
ACCESS_TOKEN = "mock-access-token"  # noqa: S105

PAGE_SIZE = 1_000  # records per NDJSON file / page


def _make_patient(index: int) -> dict:
    return {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "id": f"patient-{index}",
        "meta": {"lastUpdated": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"},
        "name": [{"family": f"Family{index}", "given": [f"Given{index}"]}],
    }


def _make_ndjson_page(start: int, count: int) -> bytes:
    records = [_make_patient(i) for i in range(start, start + count)]
    return ("\n".join(json.dumps(r) for r in records) + "\n").encode()


def _make_audit_event(action: str, entity_ref: str) -> dict:
    return {
        "resourceType": "AuditEvent",
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "type": {
            "system": "http://dicom.nema.org/resources/ontology/DCM",
            "code": "110106",
            "display": "Export",
        },
        "action": action,
        "recorded": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "outcome": "0",
        "agent": [
            {
                "who": {"identifier": {"value": CLIENT_ID}},
                "requestor": True,
            }
        ],
        "source": {"site": "fhir.example.com"},
        "entity": [{"what": {"reference": entity_ref}}],
    }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_fhir_client() -> MagicMock:
    """Return a minimal mock representing a provisioned FHIR client."""
    client = MagicMock()
    client.client_id = CLIENT_ID
    client.base_url = BASE_URL
    client.is_provisioned = True
    client.access_token = ACCESS_TOKEN
    return client


@pytest.fixture()
def http_session(mock_fhir_client: MagicMock) -> httpx.Client:
    """Return an httpx.Client pre-configured with bearer auth."""
    return httpx.Client(
        headers={
            "Authorization": f"Bearer {mock_fhir_client.access_token}",
            "Accept": NDJSON_CONTENT_TYPE,
            "Prefer": "respond-async",
        },
        base_url=BASE_URL,
        timeout=30,
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def mocked_responses() -> Generator[RequestsMock, None, None]:
    """Activate responses mock library for all tests that use it."""
    with responses_lib.RequestsMock(assert_all_requests_are_fired=False) as rsps:
        yield rsps


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper – build a full multi-page export wire mock
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def _register_bulk_export_flow(
    rsps: RequestsMock,
    *,
    total_records: int = 2_500,
    page_size: int = PAGE_SIZE,
) -> list[str]:
    """Register all HTTP interactions for a complete bulk export flow.

    Returns the list of download URLs that are registered.
    """
    # 1. Kick-off
    rsps.add(
        responses_lib.GET,
        BULK_EXPORT_KICK_OFF,
        status=202,
        headers={
            "Content-Location": EXPORT_STATUS_URL,
            "X-Progress": "accepted",
        },
    )

    # 2. Status polling – first call still in progress
    rsps.add(
        responses_lib.GET,
        EXPORT_STATUS_URL,
        status=202,
        headers={"X-Progress": "in-progress"},
    )

    # 3. Status polling – complete; build output manifest
    pages = []
    download_urls = []
    offset = 0
    while offset < total_records:
        count = min(page_size, total_records - offset)
        url = f"{BASE_URL}/bulk-data/Patient-{offset}.ndjson"
        pages.append({"type": "Patient", "url": url, "count": count})
        download_urls.append((url, offset, count))
        offset += count

    manifest = {
        "transactionTime": "2024-06-01T00:00:00Z",
        "request": BULK_EXPORT_KICK_OFF,
        "requiresAccessToken": True,
        "output": pages,
        "error": [],
    }
    rsps.add(
        responses_lib.GET,
        EXPORT_STATUS_URL,
        status=200,
        json=manifest,
        content_type=FHIR_CONTENT_TYPE,
    )

    # 4. Each NDJSON page download
    registered_urls: list[str] = []
    for url, start, count in download_urls:
        rsps.add(
            responses_lib.GET,
            url,
            status=200,
            body=_make_ndjson_page(start, count),
            content_type=NDJSON_CONTENT_TYPE,
        )
        registered_urls.append(url)

    # 5. AuditEvent POSTs (accept any number)
    rsps.add(
        responses_lib.POST,
        AUDIT_EVENT_URL,
        status=201,
        json=_make_audit_event("E", "Patient"),
        content_type=FHIR_CONTENT_TYPE,
    )

    return registered_urls


# ===========================================================================
# TEST SUITE
# ===========================================================================


class TestClientProvisioning:
    """Verify that only a provisioned client can initiate bulk export."""

    def test_provisioned_client_has_required_attributes(
        self, mock_fhir_client: MagicMock
    ) -> None:
        assert mock_fhir_client.is_provisioned is True
        assert mock_fhir_client.client_id == CLIENT_ID
        assert mock_fhir_client.access_token == ACCESS_TOKEN
        assert mock_fhir_client.base_url == BASE_URL

    def test_unprovisioned_client_raises(self) -> None:
        unprovisioned = MagicMock()
        unprovisioned.is_provisioned = False

        with pytest.raises(PermissionError, match="not provisioned"):
            _assert_client_is_provisioned(unprovisioned)

    def test_provisioned_client_does_not_raise(
        self, mock_fhir_client: MagicMock
    ) -> None:
        _assert_client_is_provisioned(mock_fhir_client)  # should not raise


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Internal helper used by TestClientProvisioning
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def _assert_client_is_provisioned(client: MagicMock) -> None:
    if not client.is_provisioned:
        raise PermissionError(f"Client '{client.client_id}' is not provisioned.")


# ===========================================================================


class TestBulkExportKickOff:
    """Verify the kick-off request and 202 response handling."""

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_kick_off_returns_202_and_content_location(self) -> None:
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.GET,
            BULK_EXPORT_KICK_OFF,
            status=202,
            headers={"Content-Location": EXPORT_STATUS_URL},
        )

        resp = httpx.get(
            BULK_EXPORT_KICK_OFF,
            headers={
                "Authorization": f"Bearer {ACCESS_TOKEN}",
                "Accept": NDJSON_CONTENT_TYPE,
                "Prefer": "respond-async",
            },
        )

        assert resp.status_code == 202
        assert "Content-Location" in resp.headers
        assert resp.headers["Content-Location"] == EXPORT_STATUS_URL

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_kick_off_request_includes_prefer_respond_async(self) -> None:
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.GET,
            BULK_EXPORT_KICK_OFF,
            status=202,
            headers={"Content-Location": EXPORT_STATUS_URL},
        )

        httpx.get(
            BULK_EXPORT_KICK_OFF,
            headers={
                "Authorization": f"Bearer {ACCESS_TOKEN}",
                "Accept": NDJSON_CONTENT_TYPE,
                "Prefer": "respond-async",
            },
        )

        sent = responses_lib.calls[0].request
        assert sent.headers.get("Prefer") == "respond-async"

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_kick_off_without_auth_returns_401(self) -> None:
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.GET,
            BULK_EXPORT_KICK_OFF,
            status=401,
        )

        resp = httpx.get(BULK_EXPORT_KICK_OFF)
        assert resp.status_code == 401

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_kick_off_passes_type_filter_parameter(self) -> None:
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.GET,
            BULK_EXPORT_KICK_OFF,
            status=202,
            headers={"Content-Location": EXPORT_STATUS_URL},
            match_querystring=False,
        )

        resp = httpx.get(
            BULK_EXPORT_KICK_OFF,
            params={"_type": "Patient,Observation"},
            headers={
                "Authorization": f"Bearer {ACCESS_TOKEN}",
                "Accept": NDJSON_CONTENT_TYPE,
                "Prefer": "respond-async",
            },
        )

        assert resp.status_code == 202
        sent_url = responses_lib.calls[0].request.url
        assert "_type" in sent_url

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_kick_off_passes_since_parameter(self) -> None:
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.GET,
            BULK_EXPORT_KICK_OFF,
            status=202,
            headers={"Content-Location": EXPORT_STATUS_URL},
            match_querystring=False,
        )

        since = "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"
        resp = httpx.get(
            BULK_EXPORT_KICK_OFF,
            params={"_since": since},
            headers={
                "Authorization": f"Bearer {ACCESS_TOKEN}",
                "Accept": NDJSON_CONTENT_TYPE,
                "Prefer": "respond-async",
            },
        )

        assert resp.status_code == 202
        sent_url = responses_lib.calls[0].request.url
        assert "_since" in sent_url


# ===========================================================================


class TestExportStatusPolling:
    """Verify polling behaviour: in-progress 202 then final 200 manifest."""

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_polling_retries_on_202(self) -> None:
        # Two in-progress then done
        for _ in range(2):
            responses_lib.add(
                responses_lib.GET,
                EXPORT_STATUS_URL,
                status=202,
                headers={"X-Progress": "in-progress"},
            )
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.GET,
            EXPORT_STATUS_URL,
            status=200,
            json={
                "transactionTime": "2024-06-01T00:00:00Z",
                "request": BULK_EXPORT_KICK_OFF,
                "requiresAccessToken": True,
                "output": [],
                "error": [],
            },
        )

        result = _poll_export_status(EXPORT_STATUS_URL, max_polls=5, poll_interval=0)

        assert result["transactionTime"] == "2024-06-01T00:00:00Z"
        assert len(responses_lib.calls) == 3

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_polling_raises_on_timeout(self) -> None:
        for _ in range(3):
            responses_lib.add(
                responses_lib.GET,
                EXPORT_STATUS_URL,
                status=202,
                headers={"X-Progress": "in-progress"},
            )

        with pytest.raises(TimeoutError, match="Export did not complete"):
            _poll_export_status(EXPORT_STATUS_URL, max_polls=3, poll_interval=0)

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_manifest_contains_output_array(self) -> None:
        output = [
            {"type": "Patient", "url": f"{BASE_URL}/data/p0.ndjson", "count": 500}
        ]
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.GET,
            EXPORT_STATUS_URL,
            status=200,
            json={
                "transactionTime": "2024-06-01T00:00:00Z",
                "request": BULK_EXPORT_KICK_OFF,
                "requiresAccessToken": True,
                "output": output,
                "error": [],
            },
        )

        manifest = _poll_export_status(EXPORT_STATUS_URL, max_polls=1, poll_interval=0)

        assert "output" in manifest
        assert len(manifest["output"]) == 1
        assert manifest["output"][0]["type"] == "Patient"

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_manifest_error_field_is_empty_on_success(self) -> None:
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.GET,
            EXPORT_STATUS_URL,
            status=200,
            json={
                "transactionTime": "2024-06-01T00:00:00Z",
                "request": BULK_EXPORT_KICK_OFF,
                "requiresAccessToken": True,
                "output": [],
                "error": [],
            },
        )

        manifest = _poll_export_status(EXPORT_STATUS_URL, max_polls=1, poll_interval=0)
        assert manifest["error"] == []


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Polling helper used by tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def _poll_export_status(
    status_url: str,
    *,
    max_polls: int = 10,
    poll_interval: float = 2.0,
) -> dict:
    for _ in range(max_polls):
        resp = httpx.get(
            status_url,
            headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {ACCESS_TOKEN}"},
        )
        if resp.status_code == 200:
            return resp.json()
        if resp.status_code == 202:
            time.sleep(poll_interval)
            continue
        resp.raise_for_status()
    raise TimeoutError("Export did not complete within the maximum number of polls.")


# ===========================================================================


class TestNDJSONPagination:
    """Verify pagination: multiple NDJSON files, correct record counts."""

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_single_page_ndjson_parses_correctly(self) -> None:
        page_data = _make_ndjson_page(0, 10)
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.GET,
            f"{BASE_URL}/data/p0.ndjson",
            status=200,
            body=page_data,
            content_type=NDJSON_CONTENT_TYPE,
        )

        records = _download_ndjson_page(f"{BASE_URL}/data/p0.ndjson")

        assert len(records) == 10
        assert all(r["resourceType"] == "Patient" for r in records)

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_multiple_pages_aggregate_correct_total(self) -> None:
        total = 2_500
        urls = []
        offset = 0
        while offset < total:
            count = min(PAGE_SIZE, total - offset)
            url = f"{BASE_URL}/data/page-{offset}.ndjson"
            responses_lib.add(
                responses_lib.GET,
                url,
                status=200,
                body=_make_ndjson_page(offset, count),
                content_type=NDJSON_CONTENT_TYPE,
            )
            urls.append(url)
            offset += count

        all_records = _download_all_pages(urls)

        assert len(all_records) == total

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_ndjson_page_content_type_is_fhir_ndjson(self) -> None:
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.GET,
            f"{BASE_URL}/data/p0.ndjson",
            status=200,
            body=_make_ndjson_page(0, 5),
            content_type=NDJSON_CONTENT_TYPE,
        )

        resp = httpx.get(
            f"{BASE_URL}/data/p0.ndjson",
            headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {ACCESS_TOKEN}"},
        )

        assert NDJSON_CONTENT_TYPE in resp.headers.get("content-type", "")

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_each_ndjson_line_is_valid_fhir_resource(self) -> None:
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.GET,
            f"{BASE_URL}/data/p0.ndjson",
            status=200,
            body=_make_ndjson_page(0, 20),
            content_type=NDJSON_CONTENT_TYPE,
        )

        records = _download_ndjson_page(f"{BASE_URL}/data/p0.ndjson")

        for rec in records:
            assert "resourceType" in rec
            assert "id" in rec

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_page_record_count_matches_manifest_count(self) -> None:
        expected_count = 750
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.GET,
            f"{BASE_URL}/data/p0.ndjson",
            status=200,
            body=_make_ndjson_page(0, expected_count),
            content_type=NDJSON_CONTENT_TYPE,
        )

        records = _download_ndjson_page(f"{BASE_URL}/data/p0.ndjson")

        assert len(records) == expected_count

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_no_duplicate_records_across_pages(self) -> None:
        total = 3_000
        urls = []
        offset = 0
        while offset < total:
            count = min(PAGE_SIZE, total - offset)
            url = f"{BASE_URL}/data/page-{offset}.ndjson"
            responses_lib.add(
                responses_lib.GET,
                url,
                status=200,
                body=_make_ndjson_page(offset, count),
                content_type=NDJSON_CONTENT_TYPE,
            )
            urls.append(url)
            offset += count

        records = _download_all_pages(urls)
        ids = [r["id"] for r in records]

        assert len(ids) == len(set(ids)), "Duplicate patient IDs found across pages"

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_empty_export_returns_zero_records(self) -> None:
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.GET,
            EXPORT_STATUS_URL,
            status=200,
            json={
                "transactionTime": "2024-06-01T00:00:00Z",
                "request": BULK_EXPORT_KICK_OFF,
                "requiresAccessToken": True,
                "output": [],
                "error": [],
            },
        )

        manifest = _poll_export_status(EXPORT_STATUS_URL, max_polls=1, poll_interval=0)
        records = _download_all_pages(
            [entry["url"] for entry in manifest["output"]]
        )

        assert records == []

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_large_export_produces_multiple_pages(self) -> None:
        total = 5_000
        urls = []
        offset = 0
        while offset < total:
            count = min(PAGE_SIZE, total - offset)
            url = f"{BASE_URL}/data/page-{offset}.ndjson"
            responses_lib.add(
                responses_lib.GET,
                url,
                status=200,
                body=_make_ndjson_page(offset, count),
                content_type=NDJSON_CONTENT_TYPE,
            )
            urls.append(url)
            offset += count

        assert len(urls) > 1, "Large export should produce more than one page"
        records = _download_all_pages(urls)
        assert len(records) == total

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_ndjson_library_parses_page_correctly(self) -> None:
        raw_page = _make_ndjson_page(0, 3)
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.GET,
            f"{BASE_URL}/data/p0.ndjson",
            status=200,
            body=raw_page,
            content_type=NDJSON_CONTENT_TYPE,
        )

        resp = httpx.get(
            f"{BASE_URL}/data/p0.ndjson",
            headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {ACCESS_TOKEN}"},
        )
        records = ndjson.loads(resp.text)

        assert len(records) == 3
        assert records[0]["id"] == "patient-0"
        assert records[1]["id"] == "patient-1"
        assert records[2]["id"] == "patient-2"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Page download helpers used by tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def _download_ndjson_page(url: str) -> list[dict]:
    resp = httpx.get(url, headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {ACCESS_TOKEN}"})
    resp.raise_for_status()
    return ndjson.loads(resp.text)


def _download_all_pages(urls: list[str]) -> list[dict]:
    records: list[dict] = []
    for url in urls:
        records.extend(_download_ndjson_page(url))
    return records


# ===========================================================================


class TestAuditEventEmission:
    """Verify that AuditEvent resources are POSTed for every operation."""

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_audit_event_posted_on_kick_off(self) -> None:
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.POST,
            AUDIT_EVENT_URL,
            status=201,
            json=_make_audit_event("E", "$export"),
            content_type=FHIR_CONTENT_TYPE,
        )

        _emit_audit_event(action="E", entity_ref="$export")

        assert len(responses_lib.calls) == 1
        body = json.loads(responses_lib.calls[0].request.body)
        assert body["resourceType"] == "AuditEvent"

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_audit_event_posted_on_status_poll(self) -> None:
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.POST,
            AUDIT_EVENT_URL,
            status=201,
            json=_make_audit_event("E", "$export-status"),
        )

        _emit_audit_event(action="E", entity_ref="$export-status")

        body = json.loads(responses_lib.calls[0].request.body)
        assert body["entity"][0]["what"]["reference"] == "$export-status"

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_audit_event_posted_on_ndjson_download(self) -> None:
        download_url = f"{BASE_URL}/data/p0.ndjson"
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.POST,
            AUDIT_EVENT_URL,
            status=201,
            json=_make_audit_event("E", download_url),
        )

        _emit_audit_event(action="E", entity_ref=download_url)

        body = json.loads(responses_lib.calls[0].request.body)
        assert download_url in body["entity"][0]["what"]["reference"]

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_audit_event_contains_agent_client_id(self) -> None:
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.POST,
            AUDIT_EVENT_URL,
            status=201,
            json=_make_audit_event("E", "$export"),
        )

        _emit_audit_event(action="E", entity_ref="$export")

        body = json.loads(responses_lib.calls[0].request.body)
        agent_id = body["agent"][0]["who"]["identifier"]["value"]
        assert agent_id == CLIENT_ID

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_audit_event_contains_recorded_timestamp(self) -> None:
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.POST,
            AUDIT_EVENT_URL,
            status=201,
            json=_make_audit_event("E", "$export"),
        )

        _emit_audit_event(action="E", entity_ref="$export")

        body = json.loads(responses_lib.calls[0].request.body)
        assert "recorded" in body
        # Must be a valid ISO 8601 datetime
        datetime.fromisoformat(body["recorded"].replace("Z", "+00:00"))

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_audit_event_outcome_is_success(self) -> None:
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.POST,
            AUDIT_EVENT_URL,
            status=201,
            json=_make_audit_event("E", "$export"),
        )

        _emit_audit_event(action="E", entity_ref="$export")

        body = json.loads(responses_lib.calls[0].request.body)
        assert body["outcome"] == "0"  # FHIR: 0 = success

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_audit_event_server_returns_201(self) -> None:
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.POST,
            AUDIT_EVENT_URL,
            status=201,
        )

        resp = _emit_audit_event(action="E", entity_ref="$export")
        assert resp.status_code == 201

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_audit_event_failure_raises(self) -> None:
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.POST,
            AUDIT_EVENT_URL,
            status=500,
        )

        with pytest.raises(httpx.HTTPStatusError):
            resp = _emit_audit_event(action="E", entity_ref="$export")
            resp.raise_for_status()

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_audit_event_emitted_for_each_page_download(self) -> None:
        total = 3_000
        page_urls = []
        offset = 0
        while offset < total:
            count = min(PAGE_SIZE, total - offset)
            url = f"{BASE_URL}/data/page-{offset}.ndjson"
            responses_lib.add(
                responses_lib.GET,
                url,
                status=200,
                body=_make_ndjson_page(offset, count),
                content_type=NDJSON_CONTENT_TYPE,
            )
            page_urls.append(url)
            offset += count

        # AuditEvent per page
        for url in page_urls:
            responses_lib.add(
                responses_lib.POST,
                AUDIT_EVENT_URL,
                status=201,
                json=_make_audit_event("E", url),
            )

        audit_calls = 0
        for url in page_urls:
            _download_ndjson_page(url)
            _emit_audit_event(action="E", entity_ref=url)
            audit_calls += 1

        assert audit_calls == len(page_urls)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# AuditEvent emit helper
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def _emit_audit_event(action: str, entity_ref: str) -> httpx.Response:
    payload = _make_audit_event(action, entity_ref)
    resp = httpx.post(
        AUDIT_EVENT_URL,
        json=payload,
        headers={
            "Authorization": f"Bearer {ACCESS_TOKEN}",
            "Content-Type": FHIR_CONTENT_TYPE,
        },
    )
    return resp


# ===========================================================================


class TestEndToEndBulkExport:
    """
    Full end-to-end tests covering:
      kick-off → polling → manifest → paginated NDJSON download → AuditEvents
    """

    @responses_lib.activate
    def test_e2e_small_dataset_single_page(self) -> None:
        total = 50
        page_url = f"{BASE_URL}/data/p0.ndjson"

        # Kick-off
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.GET,
            BULK_EXPORT_KICK_OFF,
            status=202,
            headers={"Content-Location": EXPORT_STATUS_URL},
        )
        # Status
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.GET,
            EXPORT_STATUS_URL,
            status=200,
            json={
                "transactionTime": "2024-06-01T00:00:00Z",
                "request": BULK_EXPORT_KICK_OFF,
                "requiresAccessToken": True,
                "output": [{"type": "Patient", "url": page_url, "count": total}],
                "error": [],
            },
        )
        # Page
        responses_lib.add(
            responses_lib.GET,
            page_url,
            status=200,
            body=_make_ndjson_page(0, total),
            content_type=NDJSON_CONTENT_TYPE,
        )
        # AuditEvents (kick-off + status + download = 3)
        for _ in range(3):
            responses_lib.add(
                responses_lib.POST,
                AUDIT_EVENT_URL,
                status=201,
            )

"""
pytest tests for FHIR Bulk Export requirements:
  1. A provisioned client shall request a FHIR bulk export.
  2. Large datasets shall be paginated with NDJSON output.
  3. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation, tested end-to-end.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import time
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Generator
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

import httpx
import ndjson
import pytest
import responses as responses_lib
from responses import RequestsMock

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants / helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

BASE_URL = "https://fhir.example.com"
BULK_EXPORT_KICK_OFF = f"{BASE_URL}/$export"
EXPORT_STATUS_URL = f"{BASE_URL}/$export-status"
AUDIT_EVENT_URL = f"{BASE_URL}/AuditEvent"
TOKEN_URL = f"{BASE_URL}/oauth/token"

NDJSON_CONTENT_TYPE = "application/fhir+ndjson"
FHIR_CONTENT_TYPE = "application/fhir+json"

CLIENT_ID = "test-client-id"
CLIENT_SECRET = "test-client-secret"  # noqa: S105 (test secret)
ACCESS_TOKEN = "mock-access-token"  # noqa: S105

PAGE_SIZE = 1_000  # records per NDJSON file / page


def _make_patient(index: int) -> dict:
    return {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "id": f"patient-{index}",
        "meta": {"lastUpdated": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"},
        "name": [{"family": f"Family{index}", "given": [f"Given{index}"]}],
    }


def _make_ndjson_page(start: int, count: int) -> bytes:
    records = [_make_patient(i) for i in range(start, start + count)]
    return ("\n".join(json.dumps(r) for r in records) + "\n").encode()


def _make_audit_event(action: str, entity_ref: str) -> dict:
    return {
        "resourceType": "AuditEvent",
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "type": {
            "system": "http://dicom.nema.org/resources/ontology/DCM",
            "code": "110106",
            "display": "Export",
        },
        "action": action,
        "recorded": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "outcome": "0",
        "agent": [
            {
                "who": {"identifier": {"value": CLIENT_ID}},
                "requestor": True,
            }
        ],
        "source": {"site": "fhir.example.com"},
        "entity": [{"what": {"reference": entity_ref}}],
    }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_fhir_client() -> MagicMock:
    """Return a minimal mock representing a provisioned FHIR client."""
    client = MagicMock()
    client.client_id = CLIENT_ID
    client.base_url = BASE_URL
    client.is_provisioned = True
    client.access_token = ACCESS_TOKEN
    return client


@pytest.fixture()
def http_session(mock_fhir_client: MagicMock) -> httpx.Client:
    """Return an httpx.Client pre-configured with bearer auth."""
    return httpx.Client(
        headers={
            "Authorization": f"Bearer {mock_fhir_client.access_token}",
            "Accept": NDJSON_CONTENT_TYPE,
            "Prefer": "respond-async",
        },
        base_url=BASE_URL,
        timeout=30,
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def mocked_responses() -> Generator[RequestsMock, None, None]:
    """Activate responses mock library for all tests that use it."""
    with responses_lib.RequestsMock(assert_all_requests_are_fired=False) as rsps:
        yield rsps
